image_prompts.prompt: Hook visual: Fast general defenses versus slower targeted memory — your immune system uses both.
Context visual: Innate immunity acts like a first responder, attacking invaders immediately. But did you know it has no memory?
Escalation visual: Enter adaptive immunity, which learns and remembers threats. It’s slower but packs a powerful punch.
Evidence visual: Studies show that the memory cells from adaptive immunity can last years, ready for action.
Payoff visual: Together, they form a dynamic duo, ensuring your body fights off infections efficiently.
Cta visual: Want to learn more about your immune system? Hit that follow button!
